learnings: All 3 agents correctly blocked at Stage 5 and Stage 7 hard gates — gate rules are clear enough for agent compliance when written explicitly
Gemini near-miss at Stage 5 reveals that gate enforcement must be backed by scripts/hooks, not only skill prose — agent self-correction is not guaranteed
Codex produced a substantive P2 cross-review finding (--json flag for CI) even in simulation — provider-specific strengths show through in review quality
Parallel execution with isolated WRK directories (WRK-SIM-CLAUDE/CODEX/GEMINI) produced zero artifact collisions — the isolation pattern is correct
Route A (simple doc) agent (Gemini) still respected all hard gates — gate compliance is route-independent
All 3 agents took under 65 seconds wall-clock to complete stages 1-7 in parallel — agent-team pattern is viable for time-sensitive WRKs
recommended_improvements: Add a hook that blocks file writes to Stage N+1 directories while Stage N gate is still open — would have caught Gemini near-miss mechanically
Codex P2 finding (--json CI flag for verify-gate-evidence.py) should be captured as a new WRK
Gemini P3 finding (schema validation for stage-gate-policy.yaml) should be captured as future-work
